item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This is by far my favorite SCP, the concept is so interesting as they can have so many different interactions with each other and humans, who see them as gods. All I want is some sort of tale, experiment log, or even some of the info on the specific flavors it can be.

item.title: Cool but needs more.
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP. The idea is interesting and creative, it has a dash of humor, and it would be so easy to expand upon. I'd love to see more with this, a tale or test log. Neutral vote just because of the wasted potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The moral of the story is: If you don't take care of your teeth, you don't get cool tooth civilizations.
This message brought to you by the Foundation's personal hygiene division

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image source. Got it from SCP-TH Facebook page.

http://www.illusion.co.th/Portfolio/Pages/CGI.html#42

It's advertisement image. So you should at very least link back to them.

item.title: "Suffer"
item.content:encoded: The one problem I noticed is how you said the tooth "suffers" the transformation. Being that it is no longer alive, I believe "undergo transformation" would fit much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've probably read through about 400 different SCP stories now, but this one gets my first comment. Even after various disturbing images, the tooth coliseum was the first to earn a WTF from me. Such a fun concept you've explored.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but it needs some extended info on those civilizations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be much better with flavours. Sumerian Strawberry! Aztec Apple! Mayan Mint! Persian Peppermint! Greek Grape! Chinese Cherry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I NEED MO, test logs, the names of the flavors, the mass of the Higgs boson, common man, apply yourself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one, but I do think you need to add a test log, an idea like this is too good to waste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. As soon as I noticed the parallel between the concept portrayed in The Simpsons and that portrayed here, the whole article fell apart for me.

item.title: A good scip is like chocolate, its bittersweet yet mysterious and leaves you wanting more.
item.content:encoded: Good article. Really captivated my imagination for the 5 minutes I read it. That said, now you got the hook down, you need to expand upon it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like most other people have already said, this article would be GREATLY improved with a test log to show the civilizations interacting with one another. However, on top of that, two things stood out to me:

Instances of SCP-2026-1 are capable of speaking, writing and reading in language corresponding to the flavor's ciilization.

I think a quick proofread is needed. Also, something that just confused me was how it was stated that 2026 couldn't build out of organic tissue and yet was focused on a tooth, but a re-read revealed that it was organic TISSUE, not organic MATERIAL. My bad.

No vote for now, but I'm keeping my eye on this article. A little bit of cleaning up and this puppy looks like it'll be awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sure that concept will give him something to chew on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also find it too bland. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great picture, interesting idea, but needs more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I think your suggestions would help give the article some… teeth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely like the idea, but it just needs to be fleshed out some more. It feels a little bare-bones, ya know?
The sentence structure is rough, too; it reads completely robotic because of the way you put the sentences together. You could combine a few different sentences and use some other punctuation and grammatical changes to make it read… livelier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like, but mostly because I want some.
I'm kind of intrigued, as this is normally the type of thing I'd expect from Dr. Wondertainment. I wonder if Dr. W and Wilson & Wong are aware of one another. I think an escalating competition between the two has the potential to be highly entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hehehehehehe this is so cute. Upvoted, and I concur with those who ask for a tale, this one would benefit from it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Now Scientists can play Age Of Empires in real life… with their teeth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This needs something. I don't know if it's a flavor list or some examples of societies and/or interactions and/or acts of god, but it felt like it was building up to a fun zinger, only to leave me zingerless.

Also, I'd change "suffers" to something like "undergoes". Teeth don't generally suffer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So wait, the tooth starts to grow back, but stops part way unless you brush and floss for the following three days?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean that episode of South Park where Cartman is worshiped by the sea-people. SIMPSONS DID IT! SIMPSONS DID IT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I saw the same parallel, and that ruins it for me.

item.title: Fun concept
item.content:encoded: Very interesting SCP. My only recommendation is that you get rid of the sentence in which you state that the anomalous effect only happens if the person brushes and flosses. I think that just needlessly complicates the SCP. Great job and I love that picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like that episode of The Simpson, where Lisa creates a new civilization from a tooth,some orange juice and a spark…. or maybe it's just my immagination….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I said in PM's, love the concept, and now that you posted it, love it even more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I said in chat, but more emphatically now that you've posted it, TALE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is interesting. I really want it to be longer. Test logs would be interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oy vey, the only thing I hate about this is that I didn't think of it first! I hope you add something in the way of a test log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While there are multiple mini-societies on the site, I think this one is good enough to stand with them. However, more can be done.

Currently, 15 flavors have been identified. Ten of them correspond to a type of past human civilization, while the rest correspond to imaginary ones.

Orange Ottoman! Banana Byzantine! Raspberry Roman! There's potential here.
